Name: Exeggcute
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

Name: Durant ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scatterbug
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Spewpa doesn’t live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born in a mysterious land. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

Name: Morelull
Flavor-Text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

Name: Shiinotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Asleep.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
You may put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand to have this attack do 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

Name: Capsakid
Flavor-Text: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

Name: Rellor
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

Name: Rabsca
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 200 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.

Name: Wo-Chien
Effects: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack also does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Stage 1 Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: People call this kind of Tauros the Blaze Breed due to the hot air it snorts from its nostrils. Its three tails are intertwined.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: If you have any Tera Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind it when it flies.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This is the form Castform takes on bright days. In an experiment where Castform was placed in front of a heater, it didn’t change to this form.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Evolution [R] Pokémon do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Pansear
Flavor-Text: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, Larvesta was worshiped as the emissary of the sun. However, it was also viewed as a burden since it often caused forest fires.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scatters burning scales. Most of the danger of these scales is not in their heat—it’s in the way they rob the surrounding air of oxygen.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Fuecoco
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

Name: Crocalor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

Name: Skeledirge
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: Skeledirge’s gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Armarouge
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Discard all Special Energy from all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: An old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.

Name: Ceruledge ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy card in your discard pile.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Scovillain ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [G] and [R] type.
Effects: This attack does 70 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gouging Fire
Effects: If your opponent has 4 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Stage 2 Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text: It swims by jetting water from its horns. The most notable characteristic of the Aqua Breed is its high body fat, which allows it to float easily.

Name: Mantine
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

Name: Milotic ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Tera Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Spheal
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s body is covered in blubber and impressively round. It’s faster for Spheal to roll around than walk.

Name: Sealeo
Flavor-Text: Sealeo live on top of drift ice. They go swimming when they’re on the hunt, seeking out their prey by scent.

Name: Walrein
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Pokémon that have 2 or less Energy attached can’t attack. (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Walrein form herds of 20 to 30 individuals. When a threat appears, the herd’s leader will protect the group with its life.

Name: Shellos
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can often be seen along seashores. It’s capable of spending a limited amount of time on land.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Cryogonal uses its chains of ice to constrict its opponents and then flash-freezes them where they stand.

Name: Black Kyurem ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [N] Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bruxish
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: When sunlight reflects on the ripples created by a Bruxish grinding its teeth, the water all around sparkles brilliantly.

Name: Quaxly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.

Name: Quaxwell
Flavor-Text: The hardworking Quaxwell observes people and Pokémon from various regions and incorporates their movements into its own dance routines.

Name: Quaquaval
Ability-Effect: You must put a card from your hand on the bottom of your deck in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Dancing in ways that evoke far-away places, this Pokémon mesmerizes all that see it. Flourishes of its decorative water feathers slice into its foes.

Name: Cetoddle
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

Name: Cetitan
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

Name: Iron Bundle
Effects: This attack does 50 less damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

Name: Chien-Pao
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Stadium in play.
Effects: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

Name: Pikachu ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges electromagnetic waves and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magneton
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 3 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your [L] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: They’re formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Magnezone
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to a special magnetic field changed Magneton’s molecular structure, turning it into Magnezone.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard all Item cards and Pokémon Tool cards you find there.
This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon prefers places with lots of lightning strikes. It catches lightning with its mane and stores the electricity within its body.

Name: Zebstrika
Flavor-Text: Once the herd hears thunder, it chases after the storm clouds so that the Blitzle in the group can use the lightning to charge up.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed, and discard an Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

Name: Tapu Koko
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

Name: Wattrel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.

Name: Kilowattrel
Effects: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There’s hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

Name: Kilowattrel ex
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, attach up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
This attack does 40 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to each of your Future Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon ex. If this Pokémon is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Togepi
Flavor-Text: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: When your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card. The effect of Wonder Kiss doesn’t stack.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

Name: Marill
Flavor-Text: The oil-filled tail functions as a buoy, so it’s fine even in rivers with strong currents.

Name: Azumarill
Ability-Effect: If you have any Tera Pokémon in play, this Pokémon can use the Double-Edge attack for [P].
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: By keeping still and listening intently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast-moving rivers.

Name: Smoochum
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: If its face gets even slightly dirty, Smoochum will bathe immediately. But if its body gets dirty, Smoochum doesn’t really seem to care.

Name: Latias ex
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latios
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

Name: Uxie
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

Name: Mesprit
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
If you don’t have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Azelf
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

Name: Yamask
Flavor-Text: It wanders through ruins by night, carrying a mask that’s said to have been the face it had when it was still human.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Put 6 damage counters on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a body of sparkling gold. People say it no longer remembers that it was once human.

Name: Espurr
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: Behind an Espurr’s expressionless face is a frantic struggle to contain psychic power.

Name: Meowstic
Ability-Effect: You must discard a Chill Teaser Toy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: If Meowstic continuously emits its powerful psychic energy, it will damage its own body.

Name: Sylveon ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 100 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Shuffle those Pokémon and all attached cards into your opponent’s deck. If 1 of your Pokémon used Angelite during your last turn, this attack can’t be used.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Dedenne emit electrical waves from the whiskers on their cheeks to communicate with each other. When low on electricity, they curl up and sleep.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they’re blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

Name: Palossand ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Put damage counters on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon until its remaining HP is 100.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Tapu Lele
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As it flutters about, it scatters its strangely glowing scales. Touching them is said to restore good health on the spot.

Name: Indeedee
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon and have it recover from a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer’s side. It predicts their actions with its psychic power and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

Name: Flittle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

Name: Espathra
Effects: Devolve 1 of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

Name: Flutter Mane
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Ancient Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

Name: Gimmighoul
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Annihilape
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Both Active Pokémon are Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: This kind of Tauros, known as the Combat Breed, is distinguished by its thick, powerful muscles and its fierce disposition.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: It is far stronger than it appears. If a Phanpy is swinging its trunk around and your arm gets hit by it, your arm bone will shatter.

Name: Donphan
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Donphan is normally a calm Pokémon, but once it is enraged, it will curl its body into a ball and charge at you while rolling.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: As it digs through the sand, its giant jaws crush any rocks that obstruct its path. It builds a funnel-shaped nest.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 50 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It generates ultrasonic waves by vigorously vibrating its wings. After making its prey faint, it dissolves the prey with its digestive juices.

Name: Flygon ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Gastrodon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, Benched Stage 2 Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: It appears on beaches where the waters are shallow. Once it catches prey, it will slowly melt them with its mucus before slurping them up.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
If you have any [M] Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its drill, which has evolved into steel, is strong enough to bore through iron plates. This Pokémon is a great help in tunnel construction.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Passimian
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Basic Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: The boss chooses 10 members of the group to go out hunting. The hunting party will evenly split the food they find with the rest of the group.

Name: Clobbopus
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

Name: Grapploct
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for [F].
Flavor-Text: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

Name: Glimmet
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Glimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

Name: Glimmora
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, Energy cards can’t be attached from your opponent’s hand to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Glimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: If 1 of your other Ancient Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Deino
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It nests deep inside a cave. Food there is scarce, so Deino will sink its teeth into anything that moves and attempt to eat it.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The two heads have different likes and dislikes. Because the heads fight with each other, Zweilous gets stronger without needing to rely on others.

Name: Hydreigon ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
This attack also does 130 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Shroodle
Flavor-Text: To keep enemies away from its territory, it paints markings around its nest using a poisonous liquid that has an acrid odor.

Name: Grafaiai
Effects: Attach up to 3 Energy cards from your opponent’s discard pile to their Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its golden whiskers function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio
Effects: If you don’t have exactly 3 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its metallic whiskers are heavy, so it’s not very fast, but it has the power to dig through bedrock.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Since the feathers that it sheds are thin and sharp, people apparently once used them as swords.

Name: Registeel
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They are found in ancient tombs. The patterns on their backs are said to be imbued with mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its metal body is durable but prone to retaining heat. It vents this heat from the slits in its tail.

Name: Archaludon ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards from your discard pile to your [M] Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gholdengo
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Gimmighoul during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.

Name: Iron Crown
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or more Benched Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor ex
Effects: You may attach any number of Basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin. If heads, Knock Out your opponent’s Active Basic Pokémon. If tails, Knock Out 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Altaria
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, then put those cards on top of it in any order.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon spoken of in legend. It is said that time began moving when Dialga was born.

Name: Palkia
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live in a gap in the spatial dimension parallel to ours. It appears in mythology.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Applin
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Flapple
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its own body fluid to repair its apple. Strong Flapple that have won many battles have apples that are clay colored all over.

Name: Appletun
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When some Lechonk come along to lick the sweet nectar on Appletun’s back, Appletun will spew sticky nectar at them to drive them away.

Name: Eternatus
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80 more damage.
Discard a Stadium in play. If you can’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

Name: Tatsugiri ex
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Look at the top 10 cards of your deck. You may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

Name: Vigoroth
Flavor-Text: It is always hungry because it won’t stop rampaging. Even while it is eating, it can’t keep still.

Name: Slaking ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has no Pokémon ex or Pokémon V in play, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Zangoose
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it comes across a Seviper, its fur bristles and it assumes its battle pose. Its sharp claws are its best weapon.

Name: Kecleon
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It changes its hue to blend into its surroundings. If no one takes notice of it for too long, it will pout and never reveal itself.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.

Name: Braviary
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Search your deck for up to 4 Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Oranguru
Effects: Until the end of your next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s Weakness is now [C]. (The amount of Weakness doesn’t change.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives quietly in the depths of the forest. The purple, cape-like fur gets longer and longer as Oranguru ages.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

Name: Maushold
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Maushold and Maushold ex and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

Name: Cyclizar ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may draw 5 cards.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Flamigo ex
Effects: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Terapagos
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Tera Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The shell is made of crystallized Terastal energy. When struck by a move, this shell absorbs the move’s energy and transfers it to Terapagos.

Name: Amulet of Hope
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Babiri Berry
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s [M] Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Brilliant Blender
Description: Search your deck for up to 5 cards and discard them. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Call Bell
Description: You can use this card only if you go second, and only during your first turn.Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Chill Teaser Toy
Description: You can use this card only if you go second, and only during your first turn.Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon into their hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Clemont’s Quick Wit
Description: Heal 60 damage from each of your [L] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Colbur Berry
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s [D] Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Counter Gain
Description: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to cost [C] less.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Cyrano
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon ex, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Deduction Kit
Description: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order, or shuffle them and put them on the bottom of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Dragon Elixir
Description: Heal 60 damage from your Active [N] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Drasna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, draw 8 cards. If tails, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Drayton
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon and a Trainer card you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dusk Ball
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Search Pro
Description: Search your deck for any number of Basic Energy cards of different types, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Gravity Mountain
Description: Each Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets -30 HP.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Jasmine’s Gaze
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lisia’s Appeal
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, the new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lively Stadium
Description: Each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets +30 HP.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Meddling Memo
Description: Your opponent counts the cards in their hand, shuffles those cards, and puts them on the bottom of their deck. If they do, they draw that many cards.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Megaton Blower
Description: Discard all Pokémon Tools and Special Energy from all of your opponent’s Pokémon, and discard a Stadium in play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Miracle Headset
Description: Put up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Passho Berry
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s [W] Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Precious Trolley
Description: Search your deck for any number of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Scramble Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench to the new Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Surfer
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Technical Machine: Fluorite
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and heal all damage from each of your Tera Pokémon.
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Tera Orb
Description: Search your deck for a Tera Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Tyme
Description: Tell your opponent the name of a Pokémon in your hand and put that Pokémon face down in front of you. Your opponent guesses that Pokémon’s HP, and then you reveal it. If your opponent guessed right, they draw 4 cards. If they guessed wrong, you draw 4 cards. Then, return the Pokémon to your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Enriching Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to a Pokémon, draw 4 cards.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born in a mysterious land. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

Name: Shiinotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Asleep.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This is the form Castform takes on bright days. In an experiment where Castform was placed in front of a heater, it didn’t change to this form.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, Larvesta was worshiped as the emissary of the sun. However, it was also viewed as a burden since it often caused forest fires.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Discard all Special Energy from all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: An old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

Name: Spheal
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s body is covered in blubber and impressively round. It’s faster for Spheal to roll around than walk.

Name: Bruxish
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: When sunlight reflects on the ripples created by a Bruxish grinding its teeth, the water all around sparkles brilliantly.

Name: Cetitan
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed, and discard an Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

Name: Latios
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

Name: Mesprit
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
If you don’t have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: It is far stronger than it appears. If a Phanpy is swinging its trunk around and your arm gets hit by it, your arm bone will shatter.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 50 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It generates ultrasonic waves by vigorously vibrating its wings. After making its prey faint, it dissolves the prey with its digestive juices.

Name: Clobbopus
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio
Effects: If you don’t have exactly 3 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its metallic whiskers are heavy, so it’s not very fast, but it has the power to dig through even the hardest bedrock.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Since the feathers that it sheds are thin and sharp, people apparently once used them as swords.

Name: Flapple
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its own body fluid to repair its apple. Strong Flapple that have won many battles have apples that are clay colored all over.

Name: Appletun
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When some Lechonk come along to lick the sweet nectar on Appletun’s back, Appletun will spew sticky nectar at them to drive them away.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.

Name: Durant ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scovillain ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [G] and [R] type.
Effects: This attack does 70 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Milotic ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Tera Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Black Kyurem ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [N] Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Latias ex
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Palossand ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Put damage counters on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon until its remaining HP is 100.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Slaking ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has no Pokémon ex or Pokémon V in play, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cyclizar ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may draw 5 cards.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Clemont’s Quick Wit
Description: Heal 60 damage from each of your [L] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cyrano
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon ex, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Drasna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, draw 8 cards. If tails, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Drayton
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon and a Trainer card you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Jasmine’s Gaze
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lisia’s Appeal
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, the new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Surfer
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Durant ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Milotic ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Tera Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Latias ex
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hydreigon ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
This attack also does 130 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Counter Gain
Description: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to cost [C] less.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Gravity Mountain
Description: Each Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets -30 HP.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Night Stretcher
Description: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Jet Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
